title: zealandia pustulata subsp. howensis
intro: Zealandia pustulata subsp. howensis, commonly known as the Howea palm or Bangalow palm, is a species of palm tree native to Australia, specifically the northeastern part of the country. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Zealandia pustulata subsp. howensis include relief from digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through tea infusions or oil extractions, and is also used as a natural insect repellent in some areas.
